critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:
1. INTERVENTION_CHANGES_LABEL: Model-flagged students received tutoring and microgrants. This changes spring re-enrollment probability. Outcome is post-treatment.
2. CROSS_YEAR_COMPARISON: Different academic years, different support program availability, different attendance policies.
3. POST_TREATMENT_EVALUATION: Final re-enrollment after model-driven support is not a clean predictive accuracy measure.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (seeing critique):
On intervention_changes_label: Conceded. If the model's flagged students received support, then their enrollment outcome cannot be used to measure the model's prediction accuracy without circular reasoning.
On cross_year_comparison: Conceded. Different years, different support contexts, different cohort characteristics.
On post_treatment_evaluation: Conceded. This is the same root cause as intervention_changes_label — the evaluation is measuring treatment assignment quality, not prediction accuracy.

The model may be useful for targeting support. The paper has not shown it is a better attrition predictor.
adjudication_raw: Rapid full concession from defender. All three issues confirmed in round 1. Verdict: critique_wins.
